title: J&K Assembly Debates Return of NHPC Hydropower Projects Resolution
detail: The J&K Legislative Assembly's budget session (second leg) included debate on a private member resolution tabled by National Conference legislator Sajad Shaheen, urging the Government of India to consider a phased transfer of NHPC-operated hydropower projects from central to J&K UT government control. The projects covered — Salal (690 MW), Uri-I (480 MW), Dulhasti (390 MW), Kishanganga (330 MW), Uri-II (240 MW), and Sewa-II (120 MW), totaling approximately 2,250 MW — were built by NHPC under development agreements that gave J&K preferential power supply. The resolution reflected broader J&K political discontent that the UT, despite being a major hydropower producer, faces power shortages while NHPC profits flow to the central government. The discussion occurred amid J&K CM Omar Abdullah's push to triple hydropower capacity under the IWT suspension framework.

title: Rajnath Singh: 'Operation Sindoor Is Not Over' — India Will Give Unprecedented Response to Any Misadventure
detail: Indian Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, speaking at a Sainik Samman Sammelan (soldiers' honour event) in Thiruvananthapuram on April 2, 2026, issued an unusually explicit warning to Pakistan. He declared that Operation Sindoor — India's May 2025 strikes on terrorist infrastructure in Pakistan and Pakistan-administered Kashmir — remains active and is 'not over.' Singh stated that India 'brought Pakistan to its knees within 22 minutes' during the operation and warned that any 'misadventure from across the border' would draw an 'unprecedented and decisive response.' The defence minister specifically referenced the ongoing Iran-US conflict in West Asia as a potential window during which Pakistan might attempt an operation, indicating Indian intelligence concern about adversary opportunism during geopolitical distraction. Singh's remarks came days before Pakistani Defence Minister Khawaja Asif escalated rhetoric with threats against Indian cities.
